title: arnica lanceolata subsp. prima
intro: Arnica lanceolata subsp. prima, also known as Mountain Arnica, is a subspecies of the Arnica lanceolata plant, which is a perennial flowering plant that belongs to the daisy family. The plant is native to the mountainous regions of North America and is known for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it effective in reducing pain and swelling. The health benefits of Arnica lanceolata subsp. prima include its ability to aid in the healing of wounds, reduce muscle and joint pain, and alleviate inflammation. The plant can be prepared as herbal teas, salves, and ointments, which can be applied topically to the affected area or taken internally in small doses to experience its medicinal properties.
